artifacts.role: typed shell metadata for the compiler-emitted WP_HTTP_Proxy adapter
WPHX PHP compile fixture for wp-includes/class-wp-http-proxy.php
WPHX-312.51 oracle/candidate manifest using the compiler-emitted original-path public shell
behavior receipt for the retired temporary shell candidate

claims: WPHX PHP emits the original-path wp-includes/class-wp-http-proxy.php public class adapter for WP_HTTP_Proxy.
The WPHX-312.51 candidate no longer copies and patches a public PHP class shell for promoted proxy routing behavior.

PHP still owns constants, parse_url, get_option('siteurl'), pre_http_send_through_proxy filter timing, and authentication header encoding while Haxe owns the bounded post-filter bypass routing predicate.
non_claims: This does not claim live HTTP proxy transport routing, Requests/cURL/fsockopen behavior, or TLS CONNECT behavior.
This does not claim installed WordPress distribution behavior or selected upstream HTTP PHPUnit parity for WP_HTTP_Proxy.
This does not claim WP_Http::request ownership or that WPHX PHP is a complete arbitrary-Haxe PHP backend.
